stage.name: DeFi Explained
stage.description: AMM x×y=k, impermanent loss formula, Aave health factors, yield strategies, flash loans, and MEV.

elements.content: AMM: x × y = k
Constant product formula powering Uniswap and $100B+ in liquidity

$3B TVL
Uniswap V2

x·y=k
Formula

Constant Product

Pool holds token X and Y; invariant: x × y = k is maintained after every trade

To buy Δy of Y: trader provides Δx of X such that (x+Δx)(y−Δy) = k

Price = y/x (ratio of reserves) — large trades move price more than small ones (slippage)

0.3% fee: Δx is multiplied by 0.997 before applying formula — fees accrue to LPs as higher k

Concentrated Liquidity

Uniswap V3: LPs choose a price range [Pa, Pb] to concentrate capital

Capital efficiency: a $1M V3 position in tight range = $50M V2 position in equivalent depth

Virtual reserves: position acts as if it holds more liquidity within its range only

Out-of-range positions earn zero fees — LPs must actively manage or use auto-compounders

Impermanent Loss

IL occurs when price ratio of pooled assets diverges from entry ratio

2× price change = 5.7% IL; 4× = 20% IL; 5× = 25.5% IL — formula: IL = 2√r/(1+r) − 1

IL is 'impermanent' only if price returns to entry — realized loss if you withdraw during divergence

Correlation matters: ETH/USDC pool has high IL risk; stablecoin pools (USDC/USDT) have near-zero IL
Lending: Aave Health Factors
Over-collateralized lending with liquidation mechanics

$20B+
Aave TVL

>1
Health Factor

How Lending Works

Deposit collateral (e.g., ETH) → receive aTokens that auto-accrue interest

Borrow up to max LTV (Loan-to-Value) — e.g., ETH LTV = 80% means borrow up to $800 per $1000 ETH

Interest model: utilization-based rate — rate rises steeply above optimal utilization (~80%)

Variable vs stable rates: variable follows market; stable locked at origination but can be rebalanced

Health Factor

HF = (collateral × liquidation threshold) / total borrow value

HF < 1 → liquidatable; keep HF > 1.5 for safety margin

Liquidation bonus: liquidator gets 5-15% discount on collateral to incentivize liquidation

Liquidators repay up to 50% of debt and receive equivalent collateral + bonus

Risk Parameters

LTV (Loan-to-Value): max borrow ratio, e.g., 80% for WBTC

Liquidation threshold: slightly above LTV — e.g., 82.5% (allows borrowing then price drop buffer)

Liquidation bonus: reward for liquidators — higher for volatile assets (15% for volatile vs 5% for stables)

Supply/borrow caps: governance-set limits to prevent concentration risk in a single asset
Yield Strategies
Farming, auto-compounding, and risk-adjusted returns

5-200%
Avg DeFi APY

HIGH
Risk

Basic Yield Sources

Lending interest: deposit stable → earn 3-8% APY from borrowers (Aave, Compound)

LP fees: provide liquidity → earn % of trading fees — ETH/USDC on Uniswap V3 ~5-20% APY

Staking rewards: lock tokens for protocol security — ETH staking ~3.5% APY

Liquidity mining: protocols emit governance tokens to attract liquidity — dilutive but high APY early

Auto-Compounding

Yield aggregators (Yearn, Beefy) auto-harvest and reinvest rewards

APY vs APR: APY compounds, APR does not — $100 at 100% APR compounded daily = $271 (171% APY)

Strategy risk: additional smart contract risk on top of underlying protocol risk

Convex Finance: boost CRV rewards without locking — takes protocol risk for higher yield

Risk Ladder

Lowest risk: ETH staking → Aave USDC lending → Curve stablecoin LP

Medium risk: Uniswap ETH/USDC LP + farming rewards — IL + smart contract risk

High risk: leveraged yield farming — borrow to LP, amplifies both gains and IL

Extreme risk: new protocol farming — audits may miss bugs, rug pull risk, exit liquidity risk
Flash Loans
Zero-collateral atomic loans — arbitrage, liquidation, and exploits

0.09%
Aave fee

$200M
Record loan

How Flash Loans Work

Borrow any amount in a single tx — must repay + fee before tx ends

If repayment fails, entire tx reverts — lender never loses principal

No credit check, no collateral — only constraint is returning funds in same atomic tx

Available on Aave, dYdX, Uniswap V3 — fees range 0.05% to 0.09%

Legitimate Uses

Arbitrage: borrow ETH → buy on DEX A → sell on DEX B → repay → pocket spread

Self-liquidation: flash loan to repay own loan, withdraw collateral, swap, repay flash loan

Collateral swap: replace one collateral type with another in one atomic tx without closing position

Protocol governance: borrow governance tokens, vote, repay — requires no-delay execution (mitigated by snapshot)

Exploit Pattern

Price oracle manipulation: flash loan large amount → distort AMM price → exploit dependent protocol

Example: Harvest Finance 2020 — $34M drained by manipulating USDC/USDT Curve pool price

Defense: use TWAP (time-weighted average price) oracles, not spot price — manipulation expensive over time

Reentrancy via flash loan: call external contract mid-execution to drain — prevented by checks-effects-interactions
MEV: Maximal Extractable Value
The invisible tax on every on-chain transaction

$1B+/yr
MEV extracted

60%+ blocks
Flashbots

What Is MEV

MEV = value miners/validators can extract by reordering, inserting, or censoring txs in a block

Searchers: bots monitoring mempool for profitable opportunities — compete via gas price auctions

Block builder: constructs optimal block by selecting and ordering txs to maximize fee + MEV revenue

Proposer-builder separation (PBS): validators propose blocks built by specialized builders

MEV Strategies

Sandwich attack: front-run large DEX trade (buy before), back-run it (sell after) — victim gets worse price

DEX arbitrage: two-hop price discrepancy across AMMs — pure alpha, no victim harm

Liquidation: monitor Aave/Compound for positions near HF=1, submit liquidation first

JIT liquidity: add concentrated LP exactly on the block your trade executes, collect all fees, remove immediately

Mitigation

Flashbots Protect RPC: route tx directly to miners/validators, bypassing public mempool

Private mempools: transactions not visible until included in block — MEV resistance

Commit-reveal schemes: hide tx intent until execution — prevents front-running

Batch auctions (CoW Protocol): settle all orders at uniform clearing price — eliminates sandwich attacks
DeFi Risk Framework
Quantifying and managing smart contract and protocol risks

$2B lost
Hacks 2023

Necessary
Audits

Smart Contract Risk

Reentrancy: external call before state update allows recursive draining (The DAO hack — $60M)

Integer overflow/underflow: unchecked arithmetic wraps around — Solidity 0.8+ reverts by default

Access control: missing onlyOwner allows anyone to call admin functions

Logic errors: correct code, wrong business logic — most common audit finding

Oracle Risk

On-chain oracles (AMM spot price): manipulable in same tx via flash loans

Chainlink: decentralized oracle network with 31+ nodes — aggregated median, deviation threshold updates

TWAP: time-weighted average price — resistant to manipulation but lags real price by minutes

Multi-source: use multiple oracle sources and take median — single oracle failure doesn't bring down protocol

Systemic Risk

Composability risk: DeFi Lego — one protocol exploit can cascade through dependent protocols

Governance attack: accumulate governance tokens, pass malicious proposal, drain treasury

Bridge risk: $2.5B+ stolen from bridges (Ronin $625M, Wormhole $320M, Nomad $190M)

Regulatory risk: OFAC sanctions on Tornado Cash — smart contracts can be 'sanctioned' at protocol level
The Future of DeFi
Real-world assets, intent-based trading, and institutional DeFi

$2B+
RWA market

Growing
Intent txs

Real World Assets

Tokenized T-bills (Ondo, Maple): on-chain yield from off-chain government bonds — $500M+ TVL

Tokenized private credit: commercial loans on-chain — higher yield, less liquidity, credit risk

RWA brings traditional finance yield on-chain — bridges 4-5% TradFi yield to DeFi composability

Regulatory clarity needed: securities law applies to most tokenized assets in US

Intent-Based Trading

Intent: 'I want 1000 USDC for my 0.5 ETH by end of block — fill or not at all'

Solvers compete off-chain to find best execution path — aggregates CEX + DEX + OTC liquidity

CoW Protocol, UniswapX, 1inch Fusion — shift MEV from searchers to users via solver competition

Benefits: MEV protection, better price, gas-free (solver pays gas, deducted from trade)

Institutional DeFi

Permissioned pools (Aave Arc, Maple): KYC'd participants only — institutional compliance

Real-time settlement: DeFi settles in seconds vs 2-day (T+2) TradFi settlement

24/7 liquidity: no market close, no settlement delays, global access

Remaining hurdles: counterparty risk, key management, regulatory clarity, audit standards
